design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate branching castle stages by jumping, whipping enemies, and managing limited sub-weapons while choosing companion characters that unlock alternate paths and endings
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Navigation precision under pressure + companion selection creates strategic branching; player must commit to paths that lock them into specific character abilities and final outcomes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Ancestral Belmont vampire hunter leading a ragtag band of allies (sorceress, pirate climber, dhampir) through shifting routes to Dracula's castle; each companion offers distinct tactical identity
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Returns to pure action-platforming after Simon's Quest's RPG detour; explicitly rejects non-linearity in favor of branching binary choices that permanently alter both stage design and available powers; refuses to let players respec mid-run

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with 1-up opportunities; debug menu reveals first 100 points in debug-mode games grants 1-up
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with no mid-stage saves; failure resets to stage start
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Companion choice significantly impacts difficulty curve—different characters have asymmetric movesets (Grant climbs walls, Sypha uses magic, Alucard shoots fireballs, Trevor is baseline); branching paths offer different enemy/hazard layouts; hidden name-entry codes allow players to start with 10 lives or access harder second quest

progression.upgrade_system: Companion characters function as permanent upgrades—choosing Sypha, Grant, or Alucard replaces Trevor's moveset with new abilities; switching companions mid-game requires abandonment of current partner
progression.unlock_mechanism: Branching level selection gates which companions become available; upper vs. lower route divergence after stage 3 locks player into specific castle approach; stage 6 has sub-branch in lower route
replayability_hooks: Multiple endings tied to companion selection (5 possible outcomes: solo Trevor, with each of 3 companions, or no companion at all)
Two distinct routes through castle create substantially different stage sequences
Hidden second quest accessible via name-entry code for increased difficulty
Hidden level select and stage-specific starting positions enable speedrunning/routing
Different regional versions (Japanese with VRC6 audio chip vs. Western MMC5) provide alternate soundtracks and sprite differences

world_building_techniques: Stage progression through European geography (lake crossing → bridge → castle approach via front gate OR cavern tunnels → cliff face climb) mirrors literal journey to Dracula's seat
Companion introductions contextualize allies within Gothic monster-hunting narrative (Sypha as sorceress, Grant as reformed pirate, Alucard as Dracula's own son)
Multiple endings created by companion choice reinforce moral weight of alliances—abandoning allies alters final confrontation
Branching paths structure implies strategic divergence in assault on Dracula (frontal vs. subterranean infiltration)

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-based encounters scaled to accompany choice; bosses adapt to which companion Trevor has assembled, creating asymmetric difficulty curves depending on player loadout
notable_bosses: Stage-specific mini-bosses that respond to companion abilities

faction_indicators: Sypha Belnades: sorceress faction (elemental magic user, weaker physical attack)
Grant Danasty: pirate/infiltrator faction (wall-climbing, mid-air direction change)
Alucard: Dracula's bloodline faction (internal conflict, dhampir powers of fireshot + bat transformation)
Trevor Belmont: baseline Vampire Hunter faction (whip-wielding traditional line)
danger_signals: Castle approach selection (frontal assault vs. covert infiltration) signals ideological stance toward Dracula—direct confrontation vs. stealth subversion
Companion abandonment mid-route implies abandoning allies for individual power (Grant's agility vs. Sypha's spellcasting vs. Alucard's dark heritage)
Solo run (no companion) represents solitary traditionalism of Belmont legacy vs. collaborative approach
environmental_storytelling: Lake crossing in upper route emphasizes Wallachia's natural borders as obstacle to vanquishing Dracula
Cavern/tunnel lower route evokes subterranean darkness, moral ambiguity of infiltration path
Cliff face climb echoes desperate final approach—vertically challenging stage design mirrors psychological strain
Castle interior stages progressively stack difficulty, environmental hazards (moving platforms, narrow corridors) compound combat pressure
